diagrams.title: Classification of Water-Related Diseases
diagrams.description: Shows categories of diseases caused/controlled by water
elements: Water-Borne (directly ingested pathogens): Cholera, Typhoid, Hepatitis A, Dysentery, Giardiasis
Water-Induced (vector breeding in water): Malaria (Anopheles), Dengue (Aedes), Filariasis (Culex)
diagrams.purpose: Clear understanding of disease categories and their transmission differences

diagrams.title: Fecal-Oral Transmission Cycle
diagrams.description: Complete cycle showing how water-borne diseases spread
elements: Infected person excretes pathogens in feces
Feces contaminate water source (sewage overflow, poor sanitation)
Pathogens enter gut, multiply, cause disease
Cycle repeats - infected person now excretes pathogens
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the continuous transmission cycle for prevention

diagrams.title: Mosquito Vectors and Breeding Sites
diagrams.description: Three main disease vectors with their water habitats
elements: Culex: Polluted water → Filariasis, Japanese Encephalitis
diagrams.purpose: Remember which mosquitoes breed where and what diseases they transmit

visuals.title: Water Borne Diseases Water Induced Diseases
visuals.description: Visual diagram for water borne diseases water induced diseases
